identity.title: Require production-runtime truth for product and phase completion

authorization.reference: Operator requested rule changes preventing mockups, vibe coding, scaffolding, and test-satisfying substitutes from shipping or advancing phases without working runtime behavior, then explicitly authorized implementation.

lifecycle.next_action: Implement runtime-truth authority, packet requirements, phase rewrites, fail-closed runtime gate, and adversarial negative fixtures.

scope.summary: Make operator-usable production runtime behavior the mandatory product-progress and phase-closure unit, and mechanically prevent mocks, scaffolds, prototypes, tests, or self-authored reports from substituting for the shipped artifact.
in_scope: Revise and extend Codex authority with enforceable runtime-truth rules
Add matching build rules and work-packet requirements
Replace skeleton-only product phases with runtime vertical slices without narrowing final product scope
Implement a fail-closed runtime-truth gate and integrate it into canonical verification
Add negative fixtures and unit tests for test-only, mock, scaffold, no-op, missing-artifact, and missing-counterfactual bypasses
Update the rule-to-proof map, ID registry, taskboard, and no-context model manual
Run governance, architecture, Rust, negative-fixture, and canonical PR validation
non_goals: Implementing Ferric extraction, download, archive, watcher, or FFmpeg product behavior
Claiming any Ferric runtime capability is currently implemented
Forbidding mocks or deterministic fixtures as supporting unit-test tools
Deleting final product scope or the operator-facing plugin starter-generation capability
Changing the authorized Rust plus FFmpeg and ffprobe dependency boundary
Modifying WP-FF-004 remediation source or its uncommitted work
dependencies: Existing Rust xtask and locked dependency set
Current three-root repository boundary and canonical verification gates
Cargo production profiles and process execution
No new runtime, native, test, or validator dependency

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Codex, build, and work-packet authority make operator-usable behavior from the shipped production entrypoint the only legal product-progress and phase-completion proof; tests, mocks, fixtures, reports, prototypes, and scaffolding are supporting evidence only.

The implementation sequence no longer contains a completed skeleton-only product phase: build bootstrap is a non-product prerequisite, the first product phase is a complete direct-HTTP/HLS cross-boundary runtime slice, and later phase wording requires runnable vertical outcomes without dropping final scope.

FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 validates a strict versioned runtime-proof manifest, production artifact identity, clean staged execution contract, production boundary use, observables, negative paths, and counterfactual detection; product-affecting changes cannot convert absent runtime proof into PASS or NOT_APPLICABLE.

Executable negative fixtures prove rejection of mock/test-only proof, scaffold or prototype completion, no-op success, missing artifact identity, missing clean-package execution, and absent counterfactual evidence.

Formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, full Rust tests, architecture-check, verify-pr, governance parsing, ID registry, authority mirror, allowed-path diff, and adversarial post-change review pass without claiming current Ferric runtime behavior.

checks_run: Read START_HERE.yaml, AGENTS.md, .GOV/codex.yaml, taskboard, active WP-FF-004 packet, topology, build rules, work-packet requirements/template, design phase anchors, readiness matrix, verifier implementation, and ID registry
Verified AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are byte-identical
Inspected current branch, HEAD, merge-base, dirty paths, and worktrees
Created isolated locked worktree from committed WP-FF-004 activation without copying its dirty remediation work
Checked current Rust, Cargo, Google engineering, GitHub artifact provenance, SLSA, and field Rust CLI sources
last_checkpoint.next_action: Commit activation checkpoint, then implement the runtime-truth authority and gate.

not_proven: A packaged Ferric binary, because no shipped product member exists yet
Release readiness or yt-dlp replacement parity

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: Rejected because current authority and verifier explicitly permit skeleton-only PASS.
Selected for the existing xtask process runner, reports, active-packet resolver, policy parser, and negative-fixture framework.
Selected for strict Serde validation already present, hashing, filesystem staging, process execution, and bounded checks.
Selected for Cargo production builds, Git source identity, filesystem isolation, and required FFmpeg/ffprobe preflight when scenarios activate them.
Existing serde, serde_json, sha2, and toml dependencies cover the gate; no addition is justified.
Selected as strict manifest validation plus canonical gate integration and mutation fixtures, without a new product crate.

artifact_specific_justification.codex_rules: Define the canonical prohibition and completion truth.
artifact_specific_justification.build_rules: Translate the authority into executable proof obligations.
artifact_specific_justification.packet_contract: Require runtime scenarios before product implementation and closure.
artifact_specific_justification.technical_design: Remove skeleton-only phase completion from the product sequence.
artifact_specific_justification.xtask: Fail closed when product changes omit or fake production runtime evidence.
artifact_specific_justification.negative_fixtures: Prove the validator rejects the named regression paths.
artifact_specific_justification.model_manual: Tell no-context models how runtime truth is established and recovered.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: PENDING implementation and independent RED probes

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: The gate can enforce proof structure and reject bypasses now, but no Ferric production artifact exists to execute.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: The first shipped product member or product-affecting packet activates mandatory packaged-artifact execution.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Add versioned runtime scenarios per capability and execute the exact staged release artifact through the same manifest contract.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Architecture member inventory, packet product-impact declaration, changed-path classification, and runtime-proof manifest presence fail closed.
